sentence_reviews.text: Multi-agent coordination allocates roles, information, incentives, messages, and shared resources among multiple decision-making entities.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA multiagent/game/decision topics support multiple decision-making entities and coordination; roles, incentives, messages, and shared resources combine several concepts.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets is to connect Observe–plan–act agent loops, Game theory & decision theory to downstream capabilities including Multi-agent systems & organizational intelligence, Game-playing, control & simulation agents, Graph-of-Thought & non-linear deliberation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Agent loops and game theory are supported, but the downstream atlas list is graph-derived and broader than the source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Multi-agent systems & organizational intelligence
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Multiagent systems/organizational intelligence are directly aligned with multiagent coordination.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Game-playing, control & simulation agents
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Game-playing/control/simulation agents are plausible applications of multiagent coordination.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Graph-of-Thought as downstream non-linear deliberation is not supported by the cited AIMA reference.

sentence_reviews.text: Search and self-evaluation can multiply cost while preserving confident errors when states, tools, or verifiers are weak. Its direct dependencies include Observe–plan–act agent loops, Game theory & decision theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generic search/self-evaluation limitation does not specifically follow from the multiagent/game-theory evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets uses Observe–plan–act agent loops as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Coordinating agents presupposes agent decision/action loops.

sentence_reviews.text: Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets uses Game theory & decision theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Game and decision theory are direct foundations for interacting decision-makers.

sentence_reviews.text: Multi-agent coordination, debate & markets uses Distributed systems & networking as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA 4th ed. agent and multiagent/planning/search topics; used as textbook support for agent environments, actions, search/planning, and game/multiagent concepts.
sentence_reviews.notes: Message/resource coordination can use distributed systems, but it is not necessary for all multiagent settings.

edge_reviews.notes: Multiple coordinated entities require individual agent/action loops.
Game/decision theory is core support for incentives and strategic interaction.
Distributed systems are common for networked multiagent coordination, not universal.
